mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate at extremely low levels (0.65%) relative to historical norms signals that monetary policy is highly accommodative, which creates upward pressure on the 10-year Treasury yield as bond investors anticipate future rate normalization and inflation risk. Over a 20-trading-day horizon, the exceptionally low level of FEDFUNDS drives yields higher.
failure_condition: In any 1-year walk-forward period where FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, the 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 60% of observations, or the rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and yield change is below -0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,500 thousand units indicate robust residential investment and overall demand strength, which raises inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the following month.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the resulting average 20-day yield change is non-positive or the hit rate of positive yield changes is below 50% in backtesting over the prior 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment and consumer prices drive labor cost pressures and inflation pass-through, compelling the bond market to demand higher yields as compensation for reduced purchasing power. This joint signal amplifies the positive effect on the 10-year yield over 20 trading days beyond what each series contributes individually.
regime_hint: low unemployment and moderate inflation regime
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL are above their respective medians, the average 20-day yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the interaction term in a regression is not significant (p > 0.10) over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a regime of low unemployment (UNRATE below 5.0), strong industrial production growth signals that the economy is operating near capacity, creating bottlenecks and upward inflation pressure that drives the 10-year yield higher. This effect does not hold when unemployment is high.
failure_condition: In quarters where UNRATE is below 5.0, the information coefficient between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day yield change is less than 0.05, or the average yield change when INDPRO is above its median is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: The high level of real retail and food services sales (RSAFS above 480,000 million dollars) indicates strong consumer spending, which boosts economic growth expectations and inflation, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: Following RSAFS releases above the 90th percentile, the forward 5-day yield change is negative in more than half of the cases, or the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and 5-day yield change is not positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 260, it signals persistent inflation that forces the bond market to demand higher yields to maintain real returns, driving the 10-year Treasury yield up over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, the median 20-day yield change is not positive, or the proportion of positive yield changes is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 152,000 thousand persons reflect a tightening labor market that generates wage pressures and inflation expectations, leading to an increase in the 10-year yield over the following month.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS is above 152000, the average 20-day yield change is non-positive, or the hit rate of yield increase is below 50% over a 5-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high consumer spending (RSAFS) and a very low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates that the economy is overheating with policy accommodation, which intensifies expectations of monetary tightening and higher inflation, jointly driving the 10-year yield substantially higher over a quarter. The joint effect is larger than the sum of individual effects.
failure_condition: When RSAFS is above its median and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, the average 60-day yield change is not higher than the average when only one condition holds, or the interaction term in a predictive regression has a p-value > 0.10 over a 3-year window.
